channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: NFL legend demands league address 'weaponized quarterback slide' after Trevor Lawrence injury
item.description: Pro Football Hall of Famer Joe Thomas called on the NFL to address the "weaponized quarterback slide" after the Trevor Lawrence injury.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court can take massive step in preventing trans athletes in girls' sports with historic hearing
item.description: The Supreme Court faces a decision that could help determine whether biological males will compete against female athletes for generations to come.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Auburn's Bruce Pearl applauds NYC mayor's message to critics over desire to meet with incoming border czar
item.description: Auburn Tigers men's basketball coach Bruce Pearl applauded New York City Mayor Eric Adams for challenging critics over his desire to meet with President-elect Donald Trump's border czar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iowa State and SMU athletic directors in a 'stay off my lawn' moment regarding latest rankings
item.description: Athletic directors at Iowa State and SMU are in a turf war over the latest College Football Playoff rankings, leading to a “stay off my lawn" moment on social media. The background: SMU, at 11-1 and riding a nine-game winning streak into the Atlantic Coast Conference title game, was slotted at No.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Flames honor the late Johnny Gaudreau and welcome his family back to Calgary
item.description: CALGARY, Alberta (AP) — The Calgary Flames paid tribute to longtime star Johnny Gaudreau on Tuesday night before their game against the Columbus Blue Jackets, months after the All-Star was killed while biking on a New Jersey road this summer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Knicks beat Magic to advance in the NBA Cup. Orlando also moves on as the East wild card
item.description: Karl-Anthony Towns had 23 points and 15 rebounds, Jalen Brunson scored 21 points and the New York Knicks beat the Orlando Magic 121-106 on Tuesday to reach the quarterfinals of the NBA Cup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nick Rimando elected to National Soccer Hall of Fame
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Former Real Salt Lake goalkeeper Nick Rimando has been elected to the National Soccer Hall of Fame. Rimando will be enshrined with the Class of 2025 on May 3, 2025 in Frisco, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Tom Brady explains why he has 'mixed emotions' after seeing Azeez Al-Shaair's brutal hit on Trevor Lawrence
item.description: Tom Brady gave his opinion on the Azeez Al-Shaair hit against Trevor Lawrence, which led to an NFL suspension, and he questioned why the defense is always the ones at fault.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USWNT beats Netherlands 2-1 in goalkeeper Alyssa Naeher's final match
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Alyssa Naeher ended her national team career with one last win. The stalwart goalkeeper made two critical saves in her final match for the United States, and the Americans beat the Netherlands 2-1 on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Religious beliefs put two captains in spotlight during Premier League's Rainbow Laces campaign
item.description: The captains of two Premier League teams are in the spotlight for their choices as the competition celebrates LGBTQ+ inclusion in its campaign to promote equality and diversity. Rainbow-colored captain armbands were issued to the 20 clubs for matches last weekend and the current midweek round.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Analysis: Getting benched may have been best thing that happened to Bryce Young, Anthony Richardson
item.description: On Football analyzes the biggest topics in the NFL from week to week. For more On Football analysis, head here. ___ Getting benched may have been the best thing that happened to Bryce Young and Anthony Richardson. Both second-year quarterbacks are playing well since returning to the starting lineup.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah HC falls at home to Dallas, 2-1
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Casey DeSmith made 34 saves for the Dallas Stars, who held on to defeat the Utah Hockey Club 2-1 at Delta Center on Monday. It was DeSmith's first win since Oct. 24.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Report: Michigan seeking to play Northwestern at Soldier Field in 2025
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — College football season is over for Northwestern University, but that hasn't stopped higher-ups in the administration from weighing venue options for one of its high profile matchups in 2025. According to rg.org's Paul M.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Texas safety Michael Taaffe honors Jake Ehlinger, 'feels sorry' for fan who made sign mocking death
item.description: Taaffe had strong, passionate comments regarding a sign held up by a fan in the crowd for ESPN's College Gameday, broadcasting from the Texas A&M campus the morning before the big game.
item.dc:creator: Billy Gates, NewsNation

item.title: Jaxson Stauber shuts out Vegas in season debut, 6-0
item.description: LAS VEGAS (ABC4 Sports) - Jaxson Stauber made 29 saves for his first NHL shutout, and the Utah Hockey Club defeated the Vegas Golden Knights 6-0 at T-Mobile Arena on Saturday. It was Stauber’s debut for Utah, his seventh NHL game and his first since Feb. 22, 2023, with the Chicago Blackhawks.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Kyrie Irving scores 30 to lead Dallas past Jazz, 106-94
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Kyrie Irving went for 30 points, as the Dallas Mavericks beat the Utah Jazz for a second time this season, 106-94.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Tatum scores 35, Pritchard adds 29 off the bench and Celtics outlast Bulls 138-129
item.description: Jayson Tatum scored 35 points and had 14 rebounds to lead the Boston Celtics past the Chicago Bulls 138-129 in Friday night’s NBA Cup pool game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bears fire coach Matt Eberflus after skid marked by poor decisions late in games
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Matt Eberflus was fired by the Chicago Bears on Friday, one day after botching a timeout in a loss at Detroit. Offensive coordinator Thomas Brown will serve as interim coach, the Bears said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN and ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oakland youth football team raises funds for national championship trip
item.description: (KRON) -- One of the Bay Area's top youth football programs has qualified for the 2024 Pop Warner Championship. However, the East Bay Warriors 13U team will need the community's support to help get them there.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4
